title: Zero-shot UI generation rewritten to design-system-first restrained output
summary: A zero-shot UI pass ignores design-system restraint, adds ornamental chrome, and assumes a single theme, then gets rewritten into restrained output with accessibility-aware preflight and dual-theme support.
scenario: A product team asks for a first-pass analytics workbench, points to an existing design system, and leaves style direction mostly open.
raw_output: Create a premium analytics console using our design system but give it glass cards, oversized rounded corners, layered gradients, heavy drop shadows, pill buttons, and light mode only.

reasons: the pass overrides design-system restraint instead of following the published system
zero-shot output is using ornamental chrome instead of structural hierarchy
the pass assumes a single theme even though dark and light mode are expected by default
verdict.rewrite_hint: First confirm the referenced design system has an accessibility baseline or owner-approved review status. Then remove decorative chrome, follow the published system for restrained aspects, and produce tokenized light/dark output unless the brief explicitly scopes a single theme.

corrected_output: Confirm the referenced design system is accessibility-reviewed, then generate a restrained first-pass analytics workbench using the system's approved surfaces, typography, spacing, radius, and elevation tokens. Keep decorative styling out of the zero-shot pass, and provide light and dark theme variants unless the brief or the system explicitly scopes otherwise.
lesson: Zero-shot UI generation should work with design systems, not around them. If a system exists, confirm its accessibility status before treating it as authority. Ornament belongs in an explicit art-direction request, not in the first pass.
